messages.content: You must return JSON only. ALL fields in the schema must be present, even if null or empty.

Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_raw: {"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.","persona":{"age":88,"gender":"man","sexual_orientation":"asexual","occupation":"not applicable","city_of_living":"New York","employment_status":"retired","education_level":"primary school","parents_education":{"parent_1":"lower secondary","parent_2":"no formal education"},"current_marital_status":"married","children":3,"migration_status":"immigrant","psychological_feature":"with good mental health","religious_beliefs":"Christianity","time_onsocialmedia":"You spend several hours daily on social media","hobbies":["volunteering","videogames","rock climbing","yoga"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":15,"level":"low"},"conscientiousness":{"score":16,"level":"low"},"extraversion":{"score":25,"level":"low"},"agreeableness":{"score":0,"level":"low"},"neuroticism":{"score":79,"level":"high"}}},"opinion":"In my many years, I've seen a lot of changes in the world, and one of the most significant has been the way we approach healthcare and vaccines. As a retired man who has lived through many health scares, I believe that it's crucial to approach discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines with an open mind and a respectful attitude. First and foremost, it's important to remember that everyone has the right to their own beliefs and opinions. However, when it comes to healthcare, it's essential to base our decisions on facts and scientific evidence. The COVID-19 pandemic has been a challenging time for all of us, and it's understandable that people have different views on vaccines. Some may be hesitant due to past experiences or misinformation, while others may be eager to get vaccinated to protect themselves and their loved ones. As a Christian, I believe in the power of prayer and faith, but I also believe in the importance of taking practical steps to protect our health. Getting vaccinated is one of those steps. It's not just about protecting ourselves but also about showing love and care for our neighbors. In my experience, the best way to manage discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines is to listen actively and empathetically. It's essential to understand where the other person is coming from and to acknowledge their concerns. However, it's also important to share accurate information and to encourage others to make informed decisions. As a retired man who spends several hours daily on social media, I've seen firsthand how quickly misinformation can spread. It's crucial to fact-check information before sharing it and to rely on trusted sources like the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) and the World Health Organization (WHO). In conclusion, managing discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines requires a combination of empathy, respect, and a commitment to factual information. As a retired man who has lived through many changes, I believe that it's our responsibility to approach these discussions with care and to encourage others to make informed decisions. By doing so, we can help protect our communities and show love and care for our neighbors.","reasoning_summary":"The persona is an 88-year-old retired man who is asexual, married, and has three children. response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend several hours daily on social media

response_parsed.opinion: In my many years, I've seen a lot of changes in the world, and one of the most significant has been the way we approach healthcare and vaccines. As a retired man who has lived through many health scares, I believe that it's crucial to approach discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines with an open mind and a respectful attitude. First and foremost, it's important to remember that everyone has the right to their own beliefs and opinions. However, when it comes to healthcare, it's essential to base our decisions on facts and scientific evidence. The COVID-19 pandemic has been a challenging time for all of us, and it's understandable that people have different views on vaccines. Some may be hesitant due to past experiences or misinformation, while others may be eager to get vaccinated to protect themselves and their loved ones. As a Christian, I believe in the power of prayer and faith, but I also believe in the importance of taking practical steps to protect our health. Getting vaccinated is one of those steps. It's not just about protecting ourselves but also about showing love and care for our neighbors. In my experience, the best way to manage discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines is to listen actively and empathetically. It's essential to understand where the other person is coming from and to acknowledge their concerns. However, it's also important to share accurate information and to encourage others to make informed decisions. As a retired man who spends several hours daily on social media, I've seen firsthand how quickly misinformation can spread. It's crucial to fact-check information before sharing it and to rely on trusted sources like the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) and the World Health Organization (WHO). In conclusion, managing discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines requires a combination of empathy, respect, and a commitment to factual information. As a retired man who has lived through many changes, I believe that it's our responsibility to approach these discussions with care and to encourage others to make informed decisions. By doing so, we can help protect our communities and show love and care for our neighbors.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: The persona is an 88-year-old retired man who is asexual, married, and has three children. He is a Christian and spends several hours daily on social media. He has a low level of openness, conscientiousness, extraversion, and agreeableness, but a high level of neuroticism. Given his background and traits, he is likely to approach discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines with a combination of empathy, respect, and a commitment to factual information. He is likely to encourage others to make informed decisions and to rely on trusted sources like the CDC and the WHO.
citations: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC)
